risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to the cutaway window during handling.
risk.mit: Add protective coverings during transport and storage.

risk.desc: Possibility of incorrect mating due to unlabeled connectors.
risk.mit: Label all connectors and provide clear assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate strain relief for cables.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables have proper strain relief features.

risk.desc: Potential for improper access due to lack of visible features.
risk.mit: Include visible access features such as hatches and slots.

generated_parts.label: Interstage Adapter + Separation Datum
generated_parts.spec: Separation_display mock: separation_plane_datum, clamp_band_dummy, interstage_datum, alignment_key, no energetic device or pyro instruction

requirements: Subsystem module: Interstage Adapter + Separation Datum
Separation_display mock: separation_plane_datum, clamp_band_dummy, interstage_datum, alignment_key, no energetic device or pyro instruction
Saturn V-structured, Electron-class 2-stage smallsat launcher demonstrator
Suggested material: Al 7075 + CFRP + Inconel + ablative, process: LPBF + CFRP layup
Display-only flowpath: Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only
Cutaway internal readability: Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge
Safe risk language: Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming avoidance
Stage interface datum evidence: Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys so the mock stack assembles repeatably for review

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal.
verify.rule: Ensure interfaces are defined with mutual agreement between subsystems.

verify.value: Visible locking/preload evidence is present.
verify.rule: Ensure critical joints have visible locking or preload evidence.

risk.mit: Conduct thorough verification tests on mockup structure.

risk.mit: Implement standard fit vocabulary and ensure proper clearances.

risk.mit: Clearly label subsystems as non-functional and educational.

risk.mit: Include detailed inspection windows and service access cutouts.

risk.mit: Strictly adhere to verification focus on mockup aspects.

generated_parts.label: Stage 2 Engine Display Dummy
generated_parts.spec: Non-functional stage_engine_mock with bell_nozzle_shell, cutaway_window, engine_display_cartridge slide_rail, educational label only

requirements: Removable engine display cartridge: combustion chamber dummy, bell nozzle shell, turbopump dummy in cutaway bay (non-functional)
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only
Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge
Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming
Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys so the mock stack assembles repeatably for review
